It rained bombs and it rained rain that day.
Yes, the day had been not much to speak of before. It was wet and his shoes were ruined, and then it was fire.
He came from the Bingham Tract but that's not here and that's not there.
That day showed him who he was.
I just got back two days ago from the Sandwich Islands and he is on my mind. More than usual.
He looked like the enemy but he was not.
He first did honor for this country fighting like hell to push back the tightening circle of Nazis pressing in on the lost battalion.
That was when two silver dollars in his front shirt pocket shielded his heart from german lead.
He always carried them after that for good luck but he lost them in one battle, the one he lost his arm in.
If you don't know San Terenzo, it's near Tuscany and it's where he almost fell for good.
There were too many germans and by the time he heard the hellish sound of machine gunfire, he had taken bullets right in the gut.
Right in the gut.
He didn't stop and this confused the Germans and they waited for him to fall but he stumbled forward and found the first nest.
Ammo sprayed from the Thompson submachine gun and he tossed a handful of hand grenades and that finished the job. On the first nest.
His men raced to him, said to stay, that they would continue but the led them cause that's what leaders do.
After the second nest was silenced, he fell in the mud. Loss of blood. But he had more.
He crawled toward the final bunker and when he got there,he fought like hell to stand,and when he did he cocked his arm to throw the grenade
And what happened next, his men remembered till they were old men, and told the story and most took them for liars. They told the truth.
A german took aim with is rifle grenade and struck our man's right elbow.
It paralyzed his arm and left the grenade "clenched in a fist that suddenly didn't belong to me anymore." he later said.
His soldiers, in shock moved in too help, but he told them to stop. He was afraid the useless hand that held the grenade might drop it.
The German desperately reloaded another grenade into his rifle, but he had an idea that this was the day he would die.
And it made the sound of his heartbeat louder than anything he had ever heard.
The German looked up once and he saw our man pry the grenade from his useless right hand to his left hand.
He stopped reloading.
He watched the grenade softly fly through the air, and he thought of how it reminded him of a girl pitching a softball.
And then he thought no more.
A bullet came from God knows where, hit him in the leg and he fell down the ridge and landed on the dead German boy.
He passed out the, and when he awoke he was lying on a piece of cloth that was between two sticks and he was being carried away.
His men looked to him and he used some badly needed strength to yell something that none of them forgot.
"As far as I know, nobody's called off this war yet."
When he got to the field station they cut what was left of his right arm off but there was no anesthesia and the howl of the hero was loud.
Nobody much remembers him for any of that but that don't mean it didn't happen.
It did.
And him with his family back home living in an internment camp.
Shame.
Shame

"As you contemplate the next piece of art that you will steal, know this. I'm the real deal": so says my good friend Billy Joe Shaver.
one is about a rich wise lawyer. he lived in venice and the townspeople believed he made a deal with the devil
the rumors reached his servants and they all quit. his friends heard, too, and stopped visiting.
one day a monkey showed up in his house. the lawyer was happy to have company and taught the monkey to do all the chores.
years later a man showed up to visit him. the lawyer let him in because he had no friends but the monkey.
well the visitor was secretly the lawyer's guardian angel. yeah. and he knew that the monkey was the devil in diguise.
he said "ah! i've heard you have such a wonderful monkey servant! let me see!" and the lawyer called for the monkey but he would not come.
they searched and found the monkey, who, seeing the angel, vanished in a puff of devil dust.
the angel told the lawyer "hey that was the devil. you gotta be good from now on. he wanted your soul." and so the lawyer became good.
and that my friends, is why there's an angel carved at the top of the clock tower in venice. because of that lawyer and monkey.

i've got time for one more tale from italia. this is about the farmer who was going to biella
a farmer was on his way to the town of biella. the weather was terrible and the way was difficult but he had important things to do there.
along the way, an old man greeted him and asked him where he was off to in such a hurry. "to biella" the farmer said and continued walking.
the old man followed after him to say "you should say 'god willing!'"
the farmer looked at the old man and said "god willing i'm going to biella. and if god isn't willing i'm going to biella anyway."
well it turns out the old man was secretly the lord god. what.
"if thats how it is, you'll go to biella in seven years. until then, go live in this swamp." and he turned the farmer into a frog.
seven years later the farmer turned back into a man and continued on his way toward biella.
after a while he ran into the same old man, who greeted him once again and asked where he was going.
"to biella" said the farmer."you should say 'god willing'" said the old man."god willing. if not, i can see myself back to the swamp"
then the farmer walked to biella in silence. the end.
i think there was a lot lost in translation but its cool that god sent a dude to live in a swamp for 7 years for being rude.

i used to stay up all night on the computer every day in high school. i felt tired and shitty and depressed all the time & had no idea why
turns out if you just go to sleep when you get tired, you don't feel as tired the next day. that's some revolutionary shit right there
i had severe anxiety problems up until a year or two ago. every day since about age 5 i drank caffeinated beverages from morning until night
also i took ADHD drugs & stimulant antidepressants for most of my life. and the doctors would even UP THE DOSAGE when i said i felt shitty
I just updated an old desktop I never use and forgot that Microsoft named their Siri thing Cortana lmao
of course, being hopped up on stimulants 24/7, i never had any idea when i felt hungry, thisty, or tired, and i was super manic all the time
which resulted in never sleeping, never eating right, never being hydrated, never feeling calm or any feelings besides extreme highs or lows
fucked up but true: if you don't put chemicals into your body that hype you up all the type, you don't have to alternate between hyped/sad
two years ago i quit drinking because, get this: i found a causal link between alcohol, making poor choices & feeling shitty the next day
the capitals will never play hockey again because dc will be buried under a glacier and that will be it for their cup dreams
the overarching message of the f.r.i.e.n.d.s tv show was: its not good if u have a monkey as a pet
again, pretty revolutionary shit here. if you don't drink, you don't get hangovers. you also stop doing regrettable dumb shit all the time
why would anyone rent in nyc when they could own in downtown detroit or nola for the same price
currently working on quitting weed because of the aftereffects. this one ruffles feathers whenever i mention it, but: weed hangover is real
it snow
i started noticing that i could understand some things before weed, but not after weed. or that i would have no motivation for days after
or, you know, that if i smoked weed i would have trouble breathing. which affected my sleep. and it also has stimulant effects, so, same
the point when i realized i couldn't keep doing weed all the time: i was reading a pretty-hard-to-understand book & just barely grasping it
i had the day off, so i smoked some weed. and then i picked the book back up and couldn't understand it anymore. circuit would not complete
pretty terrifying honestly. that's nothing to laugh at. that's what happens to old people when their brains stop fucking working!!! not good
also i noticed that my ddr scores suffered by a noticeable margin for about a week afterward. maybe that doesn't sound important, but...
my goals involve getting better at everything i do. do i really want to stick with a habit that actively works against that?
not to say that it didn't do anything for me, or didn't have positive effects. i like being able to zone out and have unusual ideas or w/e
but everything has tradeoffs. the question becomes: do i want to feel funny in exchange for being literally worse at everything for a week?
i mean, i don't expect to never do it again. but i have too much shit to do to get high even once a week nowadays. can't justify it at all
i don't want to sound preachy. i really think it's important to try everything in life at like, a bunch of times, until you get the idea
in fact, i don't think i would have been able to come to any of these conclusions about this stuff without doing a ton of drugs or whatever
not sure if i have an overarching point besides: everything you do has consequences & everything has side effects you might not think about
